title: Priority Guide
description: A content-first alignment document that captures the user goal, business goal, and prioritized content hierarchy for a page or screen — before layout or visual design begins. Use this format to align teams, brief AI tools, and export a shared working spec.

kind.description: Optional item marker. Omit or set to item for normal elements.

type.description: Content category label that names the role this element plays on the page.

componentType.description: Suggested UI component or pattern. Not prescriptive — use to give the team a starting point.

title.description: The element headline or label — written as real content, not a placeholder.

content.description: Description of what this element contains, communicates, or does. Use real words, not lorem ipsum.

annotations.description: Side notes that explain why the element exists, how it behaves, what it links to, or what conditions affect it.

links.description: Related pages, documents, components, or flows this element connects to.

linkedGuideId.description: Optional guide id this button or link should navigate to in a flow.

kind.description: Marks this top-level item as a one-level group.

type.description: Content category label that names the role this group plays on the page.

componentType.description: Container class component used to frame the group.

content.description: What the group contains or why the children belong together.

children.description: One-level child items. Groups cannot contain nested groups.

id.description: Unique slug identifier. Lowercase, hyphen-separated. Used for routing and file naming.

title.description: Full name of the page or screen this guide covers.

tab.description: Short display label used in the editor tab list. Optional — defaults to title if omitted.

icon.description: Material Symbols icon name for the editor sidebar. Optional.

context.description: Product area, user flow, or page type that frames this guide.

problemStatement.description: The core problem this page must solve for the user. Should name what fails or breaks down without the page, not what the page does.

audience.description: Who is using this page, their context, and what they already know or expect.

userGoal.description: What the user needs to understand, decide, or do. One clear outcome.

businessGoal.description: What the organization needs this page to accomplish alongside the user goal.

contextDetails.description: Structured context fields beyond the core brief. Typically includes decision needed, out of scope, and contributors — but any label is valid.

checkable.description: When true, the content value is treated as a trackable decision that can be marked done. Use for 'Decision needed' fields.

checked.description: Whether this decision has been resolved. Only meaningful when checkable is true.

items.description: Prioritized content elements for this page, ordered from most to least critical (index 0 = highest priority). Each item represents one content block, action, or functional element the page must include.
